Page.Width: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualWidth, FallbackValue=700}
Page.Height: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualHeight, FallbackValue=400}

Run.Text: Just because we are able to detect a contact based on a radar echo does not mean we can positively identify it as friendly, neutral, or hostile. Other detection methods must be used to classify and then identify the contact.

A radar contact emitting a known enemy radar type will be classified as Hostile due to the combination of radar echo and emissions. However, the most reliable way to identify contacts is by using visual identification (VID). You can order any unit to VID an unknown contact which will order them into visual range. Note that this can be dangerous against unknown contacts which turn out to be hostile!
